reasoning: The document extraction has high overall fidelity but contains three major defects that require human intervention. First, a header ('FACT SHEET') leaked into the text stream, breaking a sentence across a page break. Second, there is a bare URL that was not converted into a proper hyperlink, which violates accessibility guidelines. Finally, the LLM attempted to correct a typo in the source by inserting the word 'cap', which technically alters the original text and should be reviewed.

concerns.description: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
concerns.analysis: In the fourth paragraph of the first page, the source text contains an apparent typo ('A reinforced concrete was installed.'), whereas the output inserts the word 'cap' ('A reinforced concrete cap was installed.'). While this fixes a grammatical issue and likely reflects the intended meaning, it technically alters the literal text of the source document.
concerns.output_quote: A reinforced concrete cap was installed.

concerns.analysis: There is a header leakage issue that materially interrupts the reading flow. The running header 'FACT SHEET' appears at the top of both pages in the source PDF. In the output JSON, this header is extracted as an H1 at the beginning of Page 2, directly interrupting a sentence that spans across the page break ('...will be considered in' -> 'FACT SHEET' -> 'the final permit decision.').
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Page 1 ends with: "All comments received during the public comment period or at the public hearings will be considered in" and Page 2 begins with the continued sentence: "the final permit decision."
concerns.output_quote: Page 1 ends with: "All comments received during the public comment period or at the public hearings will be considered in" ... Page 2 begins with: {"type": "heading", "level": 1, "text": "FACT SHEET"}, followed by: {"type": "paragraph", "text": "the final permit decision..."}

concerns.description: 1 bare URL(s) in text not converted to hyperlinks: https://deq.nc.gov/about/divisions/waste-management/laserfiche.
concerns.analysis: bare_url_count=1. URLs appear as plain text instead of clickable links. Per NCDIT criteria, link text not wrapped in href is a major defect.

concerns.description: Page number artifacts retained: Page 1 of 2

Header/footer elements retained in output (1): Page 1 of 2
6/28/2023
concerns.analysis: header_footer_count=1. Header/footer elements are present in extraction data. This is common in multi-page government PDFs and is typically cosmetic — the renderer strips them from HTML output.

action_items: Remove the leaked 'FACT SHEET' header to restore the flow of the interrupted sentence.
Wrap the bare URL in a proper <a> tag with an href attribute.
Determine whether to keep the grammatical correction ('cap') or revert it to match the source exactly.
